methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Terranova is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Laurence Allen that has raised $7M to date. The broader robotics and manufacturing sector includes one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers, including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics. However, the immediate peer set—filtered for same stage, valuation tier, and business model proximity—remains too sparse to support median-based triangulation.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable valuation approach given data constraints. Using total raised ($7M) as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-Series A advancement yields $17.5M. This method reflects the empirical pattern that seed companies at this stage command modest premiums over deployed capital upon demonstrated product-market fit signals and pathway to institutional rounds.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $15M to $20M, with a median point of $18M. This range brackets the stage-progression output with 15% downside and upside sensitivity bounds. Key uncertainties include the absence of granular peer transaction data at identical stage and sector intersection, and reliance on a single methodological input. Further validation would require either comparable seed-round terms from similar-stage robotics companies or revenue/customer metrics from Terranova itself to anchor revenue multiple or unit economics approaches.
